question: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

graph.answer:
Excalidraw treats canvas rendering as a function of scene state plus viewport state, not as ad hoc draw calls.

**Render path**
- `App.render()` is the orchestration point. It asks `Renderer.getRenderableElements()` for the current draw set, then stores `visibleElements` and `hasRenderableElements` before composing the UI, including the canvas layers. See [`App.tsx`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\components\App.tsx#L2139) and [`App.tsx`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\components\App.tsx#L2152).
- `Renderer` memoizes render selection using a `canvasNonce` derived from the scene nonce plus the in-progress new element’s version nonce. That means the canvas redraws when the scene changes or when relevant viewport/editing inputs change. See [`Renderer.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\scene\Renderer.ts#L159) and [`Renderer.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\scene\Renderer.ts#L208).
- `Renderer` filters the scene down to:
  - non-deleted elements
  - only elements in the viewport
  - excludes the text element currently being edited
  - optionally keeps the in-progress `newElement` off the main canvas when it is rendered elsewhere
  - reorders dragged selection inside a highlighted frame during render, without mutating the scene. See [`Renderer.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\scene\Renderer.ts#L89) and [`Renderer.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\scene\Renderer.ts#L230).

**Update path**
- The canonical element store is `Scene`. Whole-scene replacement goes through `replaceAllElements()`, which rebuilds the element maps, recomputes non-deleted caches, and then calls `triggerUpdate()`. `triggerUpdate()` bumps `sceneNonce` and notifies all subscribers. See [`Scene.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\element\src\Scene.ts#L271) and [`Scene.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\element\src\Scene.ts#L303).
- Per-element edits go through `Scene.mutateElement()`. It delegates to the low-level mutate helper, compares element versions, and only triggers a scene update if the version actually changed and the element is part of the scene. See [`Scene.ts`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\element\src\Scene.ts#L409).
- `App.mutateElement()` is just a thin wrapper over `scene.mutateElement()`, so most UI interactions end up in the same update mechanism. See [`App.tsx`](C:\Users\samch\AppData\Local\Temp\samchon-graph-corpus\excalidraw\packages\excalidraw\components\App.tsx#L4789).

**Net effect**
- Scene mutations raise a scene nonce.
- React rerenders `App`.
- `App.render()` asks `Renderer` for the current render set.
- The canvas layers draw only the visible, eligible elements, and memoization prevents unnecessary redraws when nothing relevant changed.

If you want, I can also trace one concrete interaction end to end, for example dragging a shape, editing text, or resizing an element.
